name: Audit
intent: Audit a registry listing — a skill, harness, or rig — for security and trust-boundary issues, ending in a clear verdict.

input.label: the skill or tool to audit
input.hint: pick the registry listing you want vetted

pin.name: Rokha Registry Search
pin.description: Search the Rokha Registry of published agent skills, harnesses, and rigs — Rokha runs it over her own MCP registry tools.

steps.expects: the skill/tool the user wants audited (its name)
steps.instruction: Search the Rokha Registry for the skill/tool the user named as the input and fetch its real listing document (SKILL.md / server page) — the source material for the audit.
steps.produces: the listing facts + its fetched document

steps.instruction: Inspect the FETCHED document for security issues: what it accesses (tools, endpoints, credentials, network), trust boundaries, install-time behavior, and risky patterns; flag anything suspicious with the exact line/claim that triggered it.
steps.produces: findings by severity, each tied to evidence from the document

steps.instruction: Write the findings up as a concise audit brief: scope, what was actually inspected, findings by severity with evidence, and end with ONE clear verdict — adopt / try-with-care / avoid — and the single biggest reason why.
steps.produces: an audit brief ending in an adopt/try-with-care/avoid verdict
